situation: Researching whether self-improving-agent has a passive write mechanism, or requires active triggering
task: Explore the skill's hooks, memory architecture, and trigger mechanisms
initial_mistake: Dismissed hooks as no-ops without understanding description-field matching is the primary passive trigger
correction: User corrected the analysis - the system has 3-layer triggers: description matching (primary), hooks (supplementary), manual
root_cause: Incomplete understanding of Claude Code's skill loading mechanism - focused only on hook stderr behavior
key_insights: Claude Code skill description field is the primary auto-trigger mechanism
Hooks with exit 0 + stderr only go to debug logs, not conversation
Hooks with exit 2 + stderr inject into LLM context
Hooks with exit 0 + JSON stdout (additionalContext) inject as system reminder
episodic/ and working/ directories are created on-demand by LLM, not pre-created
lesson: Before dismissing a system as incomplete, understand ALL trigger mechanisms - the obvious (hooks) may not be the primary one

user_feedback.comments: User corrected initial wrong conclusion, indicating the analysis needed deeper understanding
